items.name: Kanbots
items.source_story_title: Open source Kanban desktop app that runs parallel agents on every card
items.summary: Local-first kanban desktop app for coordinating agents on every card.
items.why_included: Commenters said the local-first `.kanbots/` layout is table-stakes for adoption and compared it favorably to Vibe Kanban.
items.evidence: One commenter said the `.kanbots/` repo-local setup is table-stakes, and another described using Vibe Kanban on most projects while wishing the older project still had active development.

items.name: Superset
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Superset (YC P26) – IDE for the agents era
items.summary: Terminal-first IDE for agent workflows and long-lived worktrees.
items.why_included: Multiple commenters described using it heavily and scaling up agent sessions across many repositories.
items.evidence: One commenter said they now run 40-50 agent sessions across several repos, and the maintainer said that if it runs in a terminal it runs in Superset.

items.name: ShadowCat
items.source_story_title: Show HN: ShadowCat – file transfer through QR Codes in a Browser
items.summary: Browser-based QR file transfer tool for moving bytes without servers.
items.why_included: The thread drew praise for the fountain-code QR transfer idea and surfaced adjacent tools with real demand.
items.evidence: Commenters called the approach efficient and "cool and minimalistic," then pointed to qr-send.com and phntm.sh as related projects.

items.name: Deno 2.8
items.summary: Runtime release adding `deno pack` and packaging workflow polish.
items.why_included: Commenters highlighted practical developer value in Deno’s permission model, TypeScript support, and new packaging path.
items.evidence: People called `deno pack` a nice addition, and several commenters contrasted Deno’s permission model and TypeScript support with Node and Bun.

items.name: Slumber
items.summary: TUI HTTP client for fast terminal-based API exploration.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced a small ecosystem of adjacent API TUIs and showed strong interest in terminal workflows.
items.evidence: Commenters compared it with Verb, Resterm, and Voiden, and one said the polish makes it worth sharing.

items.name: WebSCAD
items.source_story_title: Antigravity 2.0 Tops the OpenSCAD Architectural 3D LLM Benchmark
items.summary: OpenSCAD helper for turning multicolor CAD work into Bambu Studio exports.
items.why_included: A commenter described building it to make OpenSCAD workflows easier after using Claude for script generation.
items.evidence: The commenter said they built WebSCAD after generating OpenSCAD with Claude and exporting into Bambu Studio, which made multicolor parts easier.

items.name: qr-send
items.source_story_title: Show HN: ShadowCat – file transfer through QR Codes in a Browser
items.summary: Offline-first QR file transfer PWA using fountain-code encoding.
items.why_included: The comment thread framed it as a more polished version of the same QR-transfer idea.
items.evidence: A commenter described qr-send as a more polished version of animated QR fountain-code transfer and noted that it works as an offline-first PWA.

items.name: DeepSeek V4 Pro
items.source_story_title: DeepSeek makes the V4 Pro price discount permanent
items.summary: Frontier-model pricing update making V4 Pro unusually cheap.
items.why_included: Commenters focused on the price/performance tradeoff and practical integrations with agent tooling.
items.evidence: One commenter said V4 Pro works with Claude Code, and another called V4 Flash the best performance per dollar for agentic workloads.

items.name: SWI-Prolog
items.summary: Logic programming environment recommended as a practical starting point.
items.why_included: The top comment recommended a concrete logic-programming stack instead of abstract advice.
items.evidence: The comment opened with "Just get Swi Prolog" and paired it with the Simply Logical book as the way to start.

items.name: Simply Logical
items.summary: Introductory logic programming book recommended alongside SWI-Prolog.
items.why_included: The thread’s strongest recommendation was a book-and-tool combo for getting started with logic programming.
items.evidence: The same comment recommended Simply Logical as the starting book and linked its PDF/table of contents.
